title: Neural Scaling Law with an Irreducible Floor — extrapolating to larger compute
strategies: constant baseline: predict the mean training loss (ignores scaling structure) -> ~0.1
log-linear fit L ~ p + q*log(C) + r*log(D): captures the decreasing trend, but has no irreducible floor so it slides below the true entropy floor when extrapolated to large scale
Chinchilla-style power law L = E + A*C**(-alpha) + B*D**(-beta): grid-search the two exponents, least-squares the linear (E,A,B); the modelled floor extrapolates to larger compute
alternatives: finer/continuous exponent optimisation, robust/Huber loss on the log-runs, ridge on (E,A,B), or joint (C,D) coupling terms traded against the complexity penalty
